page_content:
LBSC791 Designing Principled Inquiry (3 Credits) Critical analysis of roles of information professions and institutions in integrating theory, methods, practice, policies, and values of the ﬁeld, and applying them to the design of future information systems and services. Prerequisite: LBSC671, LBSC602, and LBSC631; and additional 9 credits of coursework applied to the MLIS degree. Or permission of instructor. Restriction: Permission of INFO-College of Information Studies.

LBSC799 Master's Thesis Research (1-6 Credits) Intensive research conducted under the supervision of a faculty member. Prerequisite: INST701; and permission of instructor. Restriction: Must be in Library and Information Science (Master's) program; and permission of instructor; and permission of INFO-College of Information Studies. Repeatable to: 9 credits.
